prompt: Write a 300-word TAM/SAM/SOM analysis for openpatent.ai's defensive-disclosure + IP-custody market. Cover: (1) TAM — global IP services market $184B/yr (PatentBolt, Anaqua, CPA Global, Clarivate, Wolters Kluwer, LexisNexis PatentSight aggregate) + adjacent AI-asset registry $42B + adjacent digital-evidence anchoring $12B = $238B, (2) SAM — the addressable slice = IP boutiques (12k firms × $180k avg annual tech spend = $2.16B) + AI startups (8,500 firms × $48k avg = $408M) + solo inventors (1.2M global × $420 avg = $504M) + gov/defense TTOs (480 offices × $1.2M avg = $576M) + IP-heavy corporates R&D disclosure (3,200 firms × $220k = $704M) = $4.35B, (3) SOM — 5-year capture = 1.2% of SAM = $52M ARR, (4) the bottom-up validation — at the $149 tier × 95k customers + $999 × 8.2k + $2,499 × 2.4k + $4,999 × 340 = $52.4M ARR matches SOM, (5) the comparable multiples — Anaqua sold to Astorg at 8.4× ARR, PatentBolt at 6.1×, so SOM × 8× = $418M exit-floor valuation. Use DEFENEOS mythic voice. Return ONLY JSON: {tam_usd:float, sam_usd:float, som_usd:float, sam_breakdown:[5 strings], bottom_up_arr_usd:float, comparable_multiples:[2 numbers], exit_floor_usd:float}.
